reviews.review_msg: Geoff is a really good person, but a horrible lecturer. He's very slow, doesn't explain things well in a lecture setting (he's much better one-on-one) and because of these reasons a lot of students aren't taught the important content that show up on the exams. If you're taking 121, take it with another prof if at all possible.

Took this course in the summer and it was a disaster.
His lectures are extremely boring and slow. The exams are extremely hard with mt 59%avg no scaling and final ~55% avg before scaling. Does not know how the final will scale by this point. Final+mt is worth 60%. No practice for the final. Prairie learn questions were not similar to the final.
Outstanding professor, explains things extremely well!

Excellent Professor, taught the content well and took time to make sure to answer questions after some hard concepts. went to his office hours and he was patient and answered my questions in a nice manner. Although there were a few assignments that was tough, the rest and the exams were alright as long as you were listening and doing the practice.

This guy is the least responsive instructor I've ever seen. He gives too long C++ assignments and does not care about his students. The assignments are almost the same as CS 225 of Illinois University. He is also quite rude when you go to his office hours. Avoid taking his course at all cost. There are many other good instructors in UBC.
Geoffery explains complicated problems really well, I really enjoyed his lectures.

Professor Tien was an absolute delight. He taught my APSC160 class at UBC and he always made sure to go through the clicker questions afterwards (solving them) so we understood the content. The first half of the course (which he taught) was enjoyable and made sense.
Interesting and fun labs. Lectures give sufficient information. Homework is completely unexplained by the lecture notes. Midterms and final can be very hard, sometimes unreasonably.

Geoffrey was an absolute legend. The man gives the most in depth, comprehensive replies to every question whether it is a tough question or a simple question. He does an amazing job explaining every concept and his Clicker questions are very thought provoking.

Geoffrey Tien could stand to be more engaging with his classes. His lectures were incredibly boring, I found myself falling asleep many times. The course is a hard one so not falling behind is important but the style of teaching made it much harder to endure.
